title: CodebaseIndexManifest
description: The persisted fingerprint of a codebase index produced by a capability-layer indexing service. Captures a Merkle tree over (file -> chunks) so that consumers can detect what has changed without re-reading content. The manifest is the contract; the underlying chunking, embedding, and vector storage are implementation details of the capability service.

index_id.description: Stable identifier for this (repo_id, ref) index. Opaque to consumers.

repo_id.description: Stable repo slug, typically '<owner>/<name>'. Tenancy boundary for the capability service.

ref.description: Git ref this index represents. Either a branch (with optional resolved commit) or an explicit commit.

commit_sha.description: Resolved commit SHA at index time. Required for kind=commit; optional otherwise.

root_hash.description: Merkle root over the sorted (path, file_hash) pairs in `files`. Stable across runs given identical inputs.

hash_algorithm.description: Hash algorithm used for all leaf and interior nodes in the Merkle tree.

embedder.description: Identifies the embedding model used so consumers can detect compatibility. Optional because some artifacts (e.g., FTS-only indexes) have no embedding.

chunker.description: Identifies the chunking strategy used to derive chunks from files. Required when `files[*].chunks` is present.

stats.description: Optional summary counters; not part of the integrity hash.

files.description: Per-file entries, sorted by `path`. The Merkle root is computed deterministically from this list.

file_hash.description: Merkle root over `chunks` if present; otherwise the content hash of the file bytes.

language.description: Detected language id (tree-sitter grammar name) or null if unknown.

chunks.description: Optional chunk descriptors. Omitted for artifacts that index whole files only.

chunk_id.description: Stable identifier within this file; typically '<file_hash>:<index>' or a node hash.

hash.description: Content hash of the chunk bytes.

symbol.description: Optional fully-qualified symbol name extracted by the chunker.
